annotations.label: Subgroup of a free group is free

anchor.snippet: any subgroup of a free group is again a free group

annotations.note: The instance `subgroupIsFreeOfIsFree` states a subgroup of a free group is free (the Nielsen–Schreier theorem).

annotations.label: Homotopy groups

anchor.snippet: homotopy groups are used in algebraic topology to classify topological spaces

annotations.note: `HomotopyGroup N X x` defines the n-th homotopy group of a pointed space.

annotations.label: Fundamental group

anchor.snippet: The first and simplest homotopy group is the fundamental group

annotations.note: `FundamentalGroup X x` is defined as the endomorphisms of `x` in the fundamental groupoid.

annotations.label: Homology

anchor.snippet: a certain general procedure to associate a sequence of abelian groups or modules

annotations.note: `singularHomologyFunctor` assigns homology objects to a topological space; the general algebraic notion is `HomologicalComplex.homology`.

annotations.label: Cohomology

anchor.snippet: cohomology is a general term for a sequence of abelian groups defined from a cochain complex

annotations.note: `HomologicalComplex.homology` gives the homology/cohomology of a (co)chain complex for any complex shape.

annotations.label: Manifold

anchor.snippet: A manifold is a topological space that near each point resembles Euclidean space

annotations.note: `IsManifold` formalizes C^n manifolds modelled on (Euclidean/normed) model spaces via `ChartedSpace`, generalizing the topological-manifold notion.

annotations.label: Examples of manifolds

anchor.snippet: Examples include the plane , the sphere , and the torus

annotations.note: The sphere (and circle) carry manifold instances and the plane is trivially a manifold, but the torus is not formalized as a manifold.

annotations.label: Knot theory

anchor.snippet: Knot theory is the study of mathematical knots

annotations.note: Only the algebraic structures `Rack`/`Quandle` (axiomatizing knot diagram moves) exist; knot theory itself is not developed.

annotations.label: Knot (embedding of a circle)

anchor.snippet: a knot is an embedding of a circle in three-dimensional Euclidean space

annotations.note: Mathlib has no definition of a knot as an embedding of a circle into ℝ³.

annotations.label: Equivalence of knots

anchor.snippet: Two mathematical knots are equivalent if one can be transformed into the other via a deformation

annotations.note: No notion of ambient isotopy / knot equivalence is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Simplicial complex

anchor.snippet: A simplicial complex is a topological space of a certain kind

annotations.note: `Geometry.SimplicialComplex` defines a geometric simplicial complex as a set of simplices closed under faces with proper intersections.

annotations.label: CW complex

anchor.snippet: A CW complex is a type of topological space introduced by J. H. C. Whitehead

annotations.note: Classical (relative) CW complexes are formalized via `RelCWComplex`/`CWComplex`.

annotations.label: Finite presentation of fundamental group of a simplicial complex

anchor.snippet: The fundamental group of a (finite) simplicial complex does have a finite presentation

annotations.note: Mathlib does not compute the fundamental group of a simplicial complex nor establish its finite presentation.

annotations.label: Classification of finitely generated abelian groups

anchor.snippet: Finitely generated abelian groups are completely classified

annotations.note: The structure theorem expresses any finitely generated abelian group as ℤⁿ times a direct sum of `ZMod (p^e)`.

annotations.label: Functoriality of algebraic topology constructions

anchor.snippet: all constructions of algebraic topology are functorial

annotations.note: Specific constructions (e.g. `singularHomologyFunctor`, the fundamental groupoid functor) are formalized as functors, but no blanket functoriality statement exists.

annotations.label: De Rham theorem

anchor.snippet: the Betti numbers derived through simplicial homology were the same Betti numbers as those derived through de Rham cohomology

annotations.note: De Rham cohomology and the de Rham comparison theorem are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Eilenberg–Steenrod axiomatization

anchor.snippet: proved that such an axiomatization uniquely characterized the theory

annotations.note: The Eilenberg–Steenrod axioms for homology theories are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fundamental theorem of algebra via fundamental group

anchor.snippet: one may easily prove the fundamental theorem of algebra

annotations.note: The fundamental theorem of algebra (`Complex.isAlgClosed`/`Complex.exists_root`) is proved, though via complex analysis rather than the fundamental group.

annotations.label: Brouwer fixed point theorem

anchor.snippet: to itself has a fixed point

annotations.note: Only the Banach contraction fixed-point theorem exists; the Brouwer fixed-point theorem for the disk/ball is not formalized.

annotations.label: Betti number and Euler–Poincaré characteristic

anchor.snippet: The free rank of the n th homology group of a simplicial complex is the n th Betti number

annotations.note: Betti numbers and the Euler–Poincaré characteristic of complexes are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: De Rham cohomology and differential equations

anchor.snippet: to investigate the solvability of differential equations defined on the manifold in question

annotations.note: De Rham cohomology and its application to solvability of differential equations on manifolds are not formalized.

annotations.label: Orientability via top integral homology

anchor.snippet: A manifold is orientable when the top-dimensional integral homology group is the integers

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Orientation` for vector spaces but no characterization of manifold orientability via top integral homology.

annotations.label: Hairy ball theorem

anchor.snippet: admits a nowhere-vanishing continuous unit vector field if and only if n is odd

annotations.note: The hairy ball theorem on nowhere-vanishing vector fields on spheres is not formalized.

annotations.label: Invariance of domain and dimension

anchor.snippet: Invariance of domain and invariance of dimension

annotations.note: Neither invariance of domain nor topological invariance of dimension is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Borsuk–Ulam theorem

anchor.snippet: identifies at least one pair of antipodal points

annotations.note: The Borsuk–Ulam theorem is not formalized (the Mazur–Ulam theorem found is unrelated).

annotations.label: Jordan curve theorem

anchor.snippet: Jordan curve theorem and its generalizations

annotations.note: The Jordan curve theorem is not formalized in Mathlib (only unrelated 'Jordan' decompositions/algebras exist).

annotations.label: Nielsen–Schreier theorem

anchor.snippet: any subgroup of a free group is free

annotations.note: The Nielsen–Schreier theorem is the instance `subgroupIsFreeOfIsFree`: any subgroup of a free group is free.
